overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination fee (2× CLF) may not be collectible in CA

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may operate competing business after franchise ends

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration venue (Minneapolis) subject to CA law (B&P Code §20040.5)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may challenge out-of-state venue requirement

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot waive Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act rights (Corp Code §31512, B&P Code §20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchise law claims survive renewal/transfer releases

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: no statement/questionnaire can waive state franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard anti-waiver protection

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the franchise agreement (815 ILCS 705/19, 705/20)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law controls termination and nonrenewal rights

overrides.override_summary: Venue outside IL is void (except arbitration may be outside IL)
overrides.why_it_matters: Litigation must be in IL; arbitration can be in MN

overrides.override_summary: Release that violates IL Franchise Disclosure Act §41 is void
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: All initial fees deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations and outlet opens (financial assurance required by MD Securities Commissioner)
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees pay nothing until the store is open — significant cash flow benefit

overrides.override_summary: Termination for bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal law (11 USC 101 et seq.)
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy protection for MD franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law may be brought in Maryland
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can sue in their home state

overrides.override_summary: General release does not apply to MD franchise law liability. 3-year statute of limitations.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: No liquidated damages or termination penalties (Minn. Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination fee is unenforceable in MN

overrides.override_summary: No out-of-state litigation, no jury trial waiver, no consent to injunctive relief (Minn. Stat. §80C.21)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee has full litigation rights in MN

overrides.override_summary: 90 days termination notice with 60 days cure; 180 days nonrenewal notice (Minn. Stat. §80C.14)
overrides.why_it_matters: Extended cure periods significantly beyond agreement defaults (10/24-hour/30 days)

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require waiver of MN franchise law rights (Minn. Rule 2860.4400D)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: Termination fee provision DELETED IN ITS ENTIRETY
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees pay zero termination fee — the most protective state override

overrides.override_summary: Enforceable only under certain conditions per ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND noncompete may be unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at nearest AAA office to authorized location (not Minneapolis)
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee arbitrates locally

overrides.override_summary: Exemplary/punitive damages waiver may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee may recover punitive damages

overrides.override_summary: Forum restriction outside RI void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims (§19-28.1-14)
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisee can bring RI Act claims in RI

overrides.override_summary: Termination without reasonable cause is unlawful (§13.1-564 of VA Retail Franchising Act)
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination must meet VA 'reasonable cause' standard

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in all conflicts
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law supersedes franchise agreement provisions

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed place
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisee arbitrates in-state

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot waive WA Act rights except in negotiated post-agreement settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest release protection — requires independent counsel

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees must reflect franchisor's reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees may challenge the $6,000 transfer fee if it exceeds actual costs

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void if employee earns <$100K/year (adjusted for inflation) per RCW 49.62.020. Independent contractor threshold: $250K.
overrides.why_it_matters: Most franchisee employees would be exempt from noncompete restrictions

overrides.override_summary: No-poach restrictions void — franchisor cannot restrict hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: WA eliminates no-poach between franchise system participants
